reviews.review_msg: Ridiculous prof. Is an elitist. Uses conflated language in lecture and assignment instructions. Stuff in lecture does not even help you. May as well borrow a book in the library and learn Java yourself. TA's were nice though.
Easy introductory to java course. Prof was OK.
WORST couse EVER EVER since I came to this university...lectures and textbook are uesless for assignments and exams...I CANNOT believe WHY I DID NOT DROP THIS XXXX Course

Awful teacher. He was not present for the first week of school because he 'did not know' he was suppose to be there. Did not teach in class, never replied his emails, and cannot understand his accent. Entire class did awful and he had to scale. I got a B+ even though I had no idea what a Java class is.

This class is better if you have previous knowledge of programming in Java.  I took this class as a refresher course , and compared to other Intro to Programming classes I've taken at other schools, you're mostly on your own here, and the lectures don't go as in depth as they should.  But I suppose for such a large class, its difficult.

Sessional instructor, originally from China. English is poor, but he does his best to explain everything in a clear manner. Very good for first time teaching a course. Only 1 or 2 classes were taught poorly. Extremely prompt in replying to emails and good office hours.

Ali is an amazing lecturer and shows great respect for his students (he even brought cookies for us). He will definitely push you. In one semester, you can go from being a total C++ beginner to a master. However, the pace is definitely reasonable. Ali is very helpful and is always available at his office hours if you have any extra questions.

Given it was his first term lecturing he was pretty successful. Mostly read off the slides. Encouraged questions during class, but would scoff at people who asked simple questions, pretty demeaning sometimes. The midterm and quizzes were fairly simple, and he would often tell us beforehand what specific questions to expect.
